data.title: Glenfarne Group's Alaska LNG Project hits major milestones, including construction, line pipe supply, and gas agreements, advancing into early execution. CEO Brendan Duval emphasizes transition from planning to building.
data.description: In Juneau, Alaska, Glenfarne Group, LLC's subsidiary Glenfarne Alaska LNG, LLC revealed significant milestones for the Alaska LNG Project. The project, majority-owned by Glenfarne, is transitioning from development to early execution for Phase One, aiming to swiftly provide dependable and cost-effective natural gas to Alaska residents. CEO Brendan Duval emphasized the shift from planning to construction, highlighting the goal of advancing the project. This progression is geared towards ensuring access to reliable energy resources for Alaskan communities.

data.title: Eric Trump claims 'Big Banks' resisting crypto legislation to maintain financial system monopoly. Legacy banks benefit from inefficiencies, hindering tech-driven alternatives, according to Trump. Delays in payments intentional to control system. Trump's full statement on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Eric Trump has stated that major U.S. banks are actively opposing cryptocurrency legislation due to its threat to their longstanding grip on the financial system. He argued that these "Big Banks" benefit from the inefficiencies inherent in traditional finance and have no motivation to support faster, technology-driven alternatives. Trump, who is the son of President Donald Trump and a co-founder of American Bitcoin Corp. (NASDAQ: ABTC) and World Liberty Financial (CRYPTO: WLFI), made these remarks during an interview with Fox Business at Davos. He criticized the monopoly held by big banks in the financial system and highlighted the intentional inefficiencies in the current system, such as the limitations on wire transfers after 5 o'clock on Fridays. Trump suggested that these delays are deliberate as big banks aim to maintain control. The full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Ethereum mainnet beats all layer-2s in daily active addresses in Jan; spike partly due to address poisoning attacks.
data.description: In January, Ethereum mainnet saw a rise in daily active addresses surpassing all layer-2 networks. Security analysts linked some of the increase to address poisoning attacks.
